name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If alignment to size 0 is satisfied for any nonzero address, the aligned predicate is vacuously permissive for a degenerate input.

name: x86_arch_inv_false
reason: If the concrete x86 architecture constant fails its own invariant, every downstream proof relying on inv is vacuously true.

name: entry_base_zero_idx_not_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_entry_base_zero_idx_not_base(base: nat)
    requires
        x86_arch_spec.inv(),
        0 < x86_arch_spec.layers.len(),
    ensures
        x86_arch_spec.entry_base(0, base, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: Indexing at 0 should recover the original base; if not, address calculations for the first entry in every directory are wrong.

name: upper_vaddr_zero_at_root
code:
proof fn phi_4_upper_vaddr_zero_at_root()
    requires
        x86_arch_spec.inv(),
    ensures
        x86_arch_spec.upper_vaddr(0, 0) == 0nat,
{
}
reason: A zero upper virtual address at layer 0 with base 0 would mean the entire x86-64 address space has zero size, making all vaddr checks trivially fail.

name: entry_size_same_across_layers
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_size_same_across_layers()
    requires
        x86_arch_spec.inv(),
        x86_arch_spec.layers.len() >= 4,
    ensures
        x86_arch_spec.entry_size(0) == x86_arch_spec.entry_size(3),
{
}
reason: Layer 0 maps 512 GiB regions while layer 3 maps 4 KiB pages; equal entry sizes would collapse the page-table hierarchy into a single flat level.
